title: preview-kit mount() renders variants and listens to postMessage
description: Implement `packages/preview-kit`'s runtime: a `mount()` function that reads a `PreviewConfig` + the target React component, renders the requested variant, and listens for `postMessage` events to live-update props from an embedding parent (the admin panel's controls panel, TASK-49).
acceptanceCriteria: `mount(rootElement: HTMLElement, config: PreviewConfig, Component: React.ComponentType, options: { variantId: string })` renders the matching variant's initial `props` into `rootElement`
If `variantId` doesn't match any entry in `config.variants`, `mount()` renders a clear error state instead of throwing uncaught

The mounted preview listens for `window.addEventListener('message', ...)` events of a defined shape (e.g. `{ type: 'systembook:update-props', props: Record<string, unknown> }`) and re-renders the component with merged props on receipt
Messages from unexpected origins are ignored (basic `event.origin` allow-list or a shared token check) to avoid arbitrary postMessage injection
A minimal unit test renders a sample component through `mount()` and confirms both initial props and a subsequent postMessage prop update are reflected in the DOM

steps.details: In `packages/preview-kit/src/mount.ts`, use `ReactDOM.createRoot(rootElement).render(<Component {...variant.props} />)` after resolving `variant` from `config.variants.find(v => v.id === options.variantId)`.
If no matching variant is found, render a fallback element with a descriptive error message instead of calling `Component` with undefined props.

steps.description: Add postMessage listener for prop updates
steps.details: Register a `message` event listener that validates `event.data.type === 'systembook:update-props'`, merges `event.data.props` into current props state, and re-renders via the same React root.
Accept an `options.allowedOrigin` (or validate against `document.referrer`) and ignore messages from any other origin, logging a warning (not throwing) on rejection.
Using a test renderer, mount a sample component with an initial variant, assert initial props render, dispatch a synthetic `message` event with updated props, and assert the DOM reflects the update.

technicalNotes: This package is built and shipped as part of the artifact the connector bundles (TASK-41) — it must have no dependency on `apps/server` or `apps/admin`, only React and the shared `packages/schema` types.
The `postMessage` contract defined here (`systembook:update-props`) must exactly match what TASK-49's controls panel sends — keep both in sync via the shared type in `packages/schema` if practical.
